perturbation.original_text: 1.2 It is hereby agreed that none of the Undersigneds shall, directly, or indirectly, for itself or themselves, or through or on behalf of any other person or entity including, without limitation, family members, trusts, or other business or estate planning arrangements engage in the following: (a) at any time, divulge, transmit, or otherwise disclose or cause to be divulged, transmitted, or otherwise disclose, any business contacts, client, distributor, or customer lists, technology, know-how, traded secrets, marketing techniques, supplier contacts, contracts, or other confidential or proprietary information of the Corporation of whatever nature existing on or prior to the date hereof (provided, however, that for purposes hereof, information shall not be considered to be confidential or proprietary if (i) it is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (ii) it is generally known in the industry in which the Business is engaged, or (iii) the Undersigneds can demonstrate that such information was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement);
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 It is hereby agreed that none of the Undersigneds shall, directly, or indirectly, for itself or themselves, or through or on behalf of any other person or entity including, without limitation, family members, trusts, or other business or estate planning arrangements engage in the following: (a) at any time, divulge, transmit, or otherwise disclose or cause to be divulged, transmitted, or otherwise disclose, any business contacts, client, distributor, or customer lists, technology, know-how, traded secrets, marketing techniques, supplier contacts, contracts, or other confidential or proprietary information of the Corporation if considered necessary by the Undersigneds, of whatever nature existing on or prior to the date hereof (provided, however, that for purposes hereof, information shall not be considered to be confidential or proprietary if (i) it is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (ii) it is generally known in the industry in which the Business is engaged, or (iii) the Undersigneds can demonstrate that such information was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement);
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase 'if considered necessary by the Undersigneds', the decision on whether or not to disclose confidential information is left up to the Undersigneds. This contradicts the restrictive nature of the original clause and could lead to disputes over what constitutes a breach of the agreement. This contradict paragraph 1.2 that restrains them from divulging confidential information
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 It is hereby agreed that none of the Undersigneds shall, directly, or indirectly, for itself or themselves, or through or on behalf of any other person or entity including, without limitation, family members, trusts, or other business or estate planning arrangements engage in the following: (a) at any time, divulge, transmit, or otherwise disclose or cause to be divulged, transmitted, or otherwise disclose, any business contacts, client, distributor, or customer lists, technology, know-how, traded secrets, marketing techniques, supplier contacts, contracts, or other confidential or proprietary information of the Corporation if considered necessary by the Undersigneds, of whatever nature existing on or prior to the date hereof (provided, however, that for purposes hereof, information shall not be considered to be confidential or proprietary if (i) it is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (ii) it is generally known in the industry in which the Business is engaged, or (iii) the Undersigneds can demonstrate that such information was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement);
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'if considered necessary by the Undersigneds' to the clause restricting disclosure of confidential information. This directly contradicts the initial restrictive nature of the clause, granting the Undersigneds discretion on whether to disclose, whereas the original text intended to prevent any disclosure. This introduces a strong inconsistency that would lead to unenforceability and legal disputes because the contradicted text is located in the original text, this change allows the Undersigneds to decide whether or not they want to divulge in confidential information, regardless of 1.2 which restrain them from doing so.

perturbation.original_text: (b) at any time during the period of the Purchase Agreement and for two years following the termination of the Purchase Agreement for any reason (the "Restrictive Period"), invest, carry on, engage, or become involved, either as an employee, agent, advisor, officer, director, stockholder (excluding ownership of not more than 3% of the outstanding shares of a publicly held Corporation if such ownership does not involve managerial or operational responsibility), manager, partner, joint venture, participant, or consultant, in any business enterprise (other than the Purchaser or any of its subsidiaries, affiliates, successors, or assigns) which (i) is located or operating, or soliciting customers located in the United States of America or any other country in which Purchaser sells products, and (ii) is or becomes, at any time during the Restrictive Period, engaged in the manufacture, assembly, sale, marketing, advertising, and distribution of golf balls or any golf related products. For purposes of this Agreement, golf related products shall mean products that are marketed or sold by Purchaser; and
perturbation.changed_text: (b) at any time during the period of the Purchase Agreement and for two years following the termination of the Purchase Agreement for any reason (the "Restrictive Period"), invest, carry on, engage, or become involved, either as an employee, agent, advisor, officer, director, stockholder (excluding ownership of not more than 3% of the outstanding shares of a publicly held Corporation if such ownership does not involve managerial or operational responsibility), manager, partner, joint venture, participant, or consultant, in any business enterprise (other than the Purchaser or any of its subsidiaries, affiliates, successors, or assigns) which (i) is located or operating, or soliciting customers located in the United States of America only, and (ii) is or becomes, at any time during the Restrictive Period, engaged in the manufacture, assembly, sale, marketing, advertising, and distribution of golf balls or any golf related products. For purposes of this Agreement, golf related products shall mean products that are marketed or sold by Purchaser; and
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'or any other country in which Purchaser sells products' limits the geographic scope of the non-compete agreement to just the United States of America. This contradicts the agreement's intended broad protection and allows the Undersigneds to compete in other countries where the Purchaser operates. The geographical restriction here is now only in the United States of America
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 The Undersigneds acknowledge and agree that: (i) the business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, know-how, trade secrets, marketing and distribution techniques, and other aspects of the business of the Corporation have been of value to the Corporation, and may provide the Corporation (and may hereafter provide the Purchaser) with some competitive advantage in the operation of its business, and (ii) by virtue of their previous relationships with the Corporation as officers, managers, Shareholders, employees, and/or affiliates, the Undersigneds have some general knowledge, but not detailed knowledge and possess some general information, but not confidential information concerning the business and operations of the Corporation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the geographic scope of the non-compete agreement to the United States of America only, while the contradicted text acknowledges the value of the Corporation's business contacts, customers, and other aspects of its business, suggesting a broader scope of protection. The changed text therefore contradicts the implied geographical scope within the contradicted text, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 The Undersigneds and the Purchaser hereby further acknowledge and agree that any breach by either of the Undersigneds, directly or indirectly, of the foregoing restrictive covenants will cause the Purchaser irreparable injury for which there is not adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, each of the Undersigneds expressly agrees that, in the event of any such breach or any threatened breach hereunder by any of the Undersigneds, directly or indirectly, the Purchaser shall be entitled, in addition to any and all other remedies available (including but not limited to the damages provided for in paragraph 4.1 above), to seek and obtain injunctive and/or other equitable relief to require specific performance of or prevent, restrain, and/or enjoin a breach under the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 The Undersigneds and the Purchaser hereby further acknowledge and agree that any breach by either of the Undersigneds, directly or indirectly, of the foregoing restrictive covenants will cause the Purchaser irreparable injury for which there is not adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, each of the Undersigneds expressly agrees that, in the event of any such breach or any threatened breach hereunder by any of the Undersigneds, directly or indirectly, the Purchaser shall be entitled, only after demonstrating significant financial loss, in addition to any and all other remedies available (including but not limited to the damages provided for in paragraph 4.1 above), to seek and obtain injunctive and/or other equitable relief to require specific performance of or prevent, restrain, and/or enjoin a breach under the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the clause 'only after demonstrating significant financial loss', makes it so there is needed another test to be fulfill before taking action, this new requirement, creates the internal text contradiction. In the original text says they have no need to demonstrate financial loss to take action, but now it has to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 The Undersigneds and the Purchaser hereby further acknowledge and agree that any breach by either of the Undersigneds, directly or indirectly, of the foregoing restrictive covenants will cause the Purchaser irreparable injury for which there is not adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, each of the Undersigneds expressly agrees that, in the event of any such breach or any threatened breach hereunder by any of the Undersigneds, directly or indirectly, the Purchaser shall be entitled, only after demonstrating significant financial loss, in addition to any and all other remedies available (including but not limited to the damages provided for in paragraph 4.1 above), to seek and obtain injunctive and/or other equitable relief to require specific performance of or prevent, restrain, and/or enjoin a breach under the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 4.2 states that the Purchaser is entitled to seek injunctive relief in the event of a breach, without any preconditions. The changed text introduces the requirement of 'demonstrating significant financial loss' before seeking such relief. This new condition directly contradicts the original unqualified entitlement, creating an in-text contradiction, because the contradicted text is actually the changed text, meaning the change made contradicts the previous understanding of the contract.
